summary: A legendary Dark Star > Dew performance captured on a mixed‑quality audience tape; the music shines despite rough sound.
review: The show is celebrated for its epic Dark Star → Dew sequence, with many reviewers calling it one of the best ’72 outings and praising Jerry’s wailing guitar and Phil’s thunderous bass. The recording is an audience (AUD) capture that alternates between Bear‑recorded sections of surprising clarity and a murkier first set that some listeners find challenging. Overall the musical merit outweighs the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the music highly (especially the Dark Star > Dew) while noting variable sound quality; weighting show quality heavily yields a solid above‑average score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bear’s audience recording of later portions is clear and top‑tier for ’72, but the early set is muddy, with cassette hiss and occasional dropouts that make listening a chore for some.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set revolves around an extended Dark Star → Dew jam that many consider the highlight; aside from that, the performance is solid but lacks other standout moments according to a few reviewers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry’s wailing guitar and perfect tone during the Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is described as thunderous at its best.
